mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average forward return over 60 days, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the correlation between industrial production growth and short-term equity returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following inflation spikes above the threshold, or the predictive accuracy of the signal falls below 45% over a 10-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the signal generates negative alpha during periods of wage-price spirals.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses direction during demand shocks, or the combined proxy fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising federal funds rates indicates a tightening financial environment constraining manufacturing output, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns during tightening phases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when tested against a panel of 500 large-cap equities.
